symptom: inventory_snapshot compute cost spiked noticeably starting Dec 29
root_cause: A recent change added an unindexed join against raw_warehouse_events, which used to be small but has grown past the point where the warehouse can do a full scan efficiently within the existing warehouse size.
fix_applied: Added a clustering key / index on the join column in raw_warehouse_events, and split the inventory_snapshot model into an incremental model instead of a full rebuild on every run.

symptom: Airflow DAG for customer_360 stuck in 'running' state past its SLA
root_cause: A recent change added an unindexed join against marketing_events, which used to be small but has grown past the point where the warehouse can do a full scan efficiently within the existing warehouse size.
fix_applied: Added a clustering key / index on the join column in marketing_events, and split the customer_360 model into an incremental model instead of a full rebuild on every run.

symptom: Customers reporting blank region_code field in the orders_fact dashboard
root_cause: An upstream schema change in raw_orders renamed/deprecated the source field feeding region_code, but the dbt model for orders_fact still references the old column name, so the join silently returns null instead of failing.
fix_applied: Updated the orders_fact dbt model to reference the renamed column (region_code_v1 -> region_code), added a not_null dbt test on region_code, and added a schema-change Slack alert on raw_orders.

symptom: Recurring failure on customer_360 -- this is the 1st time this exact error has shown up
root_cause: marketing_events has a scheduled maintenance/vacuum window on Thursday mornings that briefly locks the table; customer_360 isn't configured to retry on lock-timeout errors, so the whole run fails instead of waiting and retrying.
fix_applied: Added retry-with-backoff logic to the customer_360 extraction step specifically for lock-timeout errors, and moved the customer_360 schedule to start 30 minutes after the maintenance window instead of overlapping it.

symptom: customer_360 job timing out / running 3-4x longer than usual since Jan 29
root_cause: A recent change added an unindexed join against marketing_events, which used to be small but has grown past the point where the warehouse can do a full scan efficiently within the existing warehouse size.
fix_applied: Added a clustering key / index on the join column in marketing_events, and split the customer_360 model into an incremental model instead of a full rebuild on every run.

symptom: Airflow DAG for orders_fact stuck in 'running' state past its SLA
root_cause: A recent change added an unindexed join against raw_orders, which used to be small but has grown past the point where the warehouse can do a full scan efficiently within the existing warehouse size.
fix_applied: Added a clustering key / index on the join column in raw_orders, and split the orders_fact model into an incremental model instead of a full rebuild on every run.

symptom: inventory_snapshot run failed at Feb 05 with a duplicate key / primary key violation
root_cause: A backfill job for raw_warehouse_events was re-run without a delete-before-insert step, so the Feb 05 window of records got inserted twice into inventory_snapshot, breaking the uniqueness assumption on event_id.
fix_applied: Added an idempotent MERGE (instead of INSERT) in the inventory_snapshot load step, and added a row-count anomaly test that compares against a 7-day rolling average before the backfill window.

symptom: finance_revenue_recognition job timing out / running 3-4x longer than usual since Feb 19
root_cause: A recent change added an unindexed join against orders_fact, which used to be small but has grown past the point where the warehouse can do a full scan efficiently within the existing warehouse size.
fix_applied: Added a clustering key / index on the join column in orders_fact, and split the finance_revenue_recognition model into an incremental model instead of a full rebuild on every run.

symptom: inventory_snapshot showing 40% null values in the region_code column since Apr 16

symptom: orders_fact numbers don't reconcile with raw_customers -- off by roughly 40%
root_cause: A metric definition change made 3 sprints ago to raw_customers (a new business rule for what counts as a 'completed' record) was never propagated to the orders_fact model, so it's still using the old filter logic and silently undercounting.
fix_applied: Aligned the orders_fact filter logic with the updated raw_customers business rule, and added an inline dbt model comment + a `metric_definition_version` column so future changes are tracked instead of tribal knowledge.

symptom: orders_fact failing every Wednesday morning, same error each time
root_cause: raw_orders has a scheduled maintenance/vacuum window on Wednesday mornings that briefly locks the table; orders_fact isn't configured to retry on lock-timeout errors, so the whole run fails instead of waiting and retrying.
fix_applied: Added retry-with-backoff logic to the orders_fact extraction step specifically for lock-timeout errors, and moved the orders_fact schedule to start 30 minutes after the maintenance window instead of overlapping it.

symptom: Customers reporting blank customer_email field in the finance_revenue_recognition dashboard
root_cause: An upstream schema change in orders_fact renamed/deprecated the source field feeding customer_email, but the dbt model for finance_revenue_recognition still references the old column name, so the join silently returns null instead of failing.
fix_applied: Updated the finance_revenue_recognition dbt model to reference the renamed column (customer_email_v1 -> customer_email), added a not_null dbt test on customer_email, and added a schema-change Slack alert on orders_fact.
